steps.title: Read the coefficient
steps.content: In $d=16t$, distance is measured in inches and time is measured in seconds. The coefficient of $t$ tells how many inches the object moves each second, so it is a rate rather than a fixed total distance.

steps.title: Interpret the rate
steps.content: For every increase of $1$ second, the distance increases by $16$ inches. At $t=1$, the distance is $16$ inches; at $t=2$, the distance is $32$ inches. So $16$ represents a rate of $16$ inches per second. The answer is C.
